entries.title: My Message in a Bottle for The Internet
entries.description:
I made this as a message in a bottle for The Archive.

Not because I think the internet is going to save us. Not because I think the platforms are suddenly going to grow a soul and stop grinding everyone into engagement powder. But because somewhere under all the rot, automation, loneliness, dead links, AI sludge, and algorithmic neglect, there are still people trying to find each other.

This is about looking for life and community in the face of structural abandonment.

A little field note from the forest.
A little signal flare from the weird web.
A little proof that I was here, thinking about all of this, before the machine filed it under “content.”

https://theinternetisdead.org

#HonestlyThomas #TheInternetIsDead #TheArchive #DeadInternetTheory #HauntedInternet #InternetArt #DigitalDada

entries.title: Structural Abandonment
entries.description:
Being isolated all day with no real community is not a personal failure. It is structural abandonment.

When people say “go to the library” but there are no groups, “go for a walk” when there is nowhere to belong, or “reach out” when every support turns into a waitlist or a pamphlet, they are not solving the problem. They are reframing the absence of support as an attitude problem.

This short is about what it feels like to be left without a real place, role, or community, then blamed for collapsing under conditions you did not create.

#HonestlyThomas #TheInternetIsDead #StructuralAbandonment #MentalHealth #Neurodivergent #DisabilityJustice #Loneliness #Community #YouTubeShorts

entries.title: Tektite - Get Up And Do It (Official Lyric Video)
entries.description:
An unreleased Tektite track from 2017, pulled out of the archive and left raw on purpose.

“Get Up And Do It” is a self-motivation transmission from an earlier version of me — back when the internet still felt like a place you could build something weird in, not just a haunted mall full of optimized thumbnails and dead-eyed engagement sludge.

This one is about discipline, doubt, persistence, haters, creative hunger, and trying to keep moving when the maze keeps rearranging itself. It is about writing anyway. Making anyway. Failing anyway. Getting back up anyway.

It is rough around the edges because that is where the fingerprints are.

A recovered signal from 2017.
Tektite before the dust settled.
Honestly Thomas before the internet finished decomposing.

https://theinternetisdead.org

entries.title: Say Something, I’m Giving Up On Views
entries.description:
I know everyone feels alone. I know everyone is exhausted, alienated, overstimulated, and trapped in their own private version of the dead mall. That is part of the horror. The difference is that I am shouting my soul into an empty room where people treat my feelings like entertainment, noise, content, or some weird spectacle to silently consume.

This video is about what it feels like to keep making signals into a world that mostly answers with silence.

Not because I need “views” as a vanity metric. Views are not witness. Views are not understanding. Views are not someone doing the work to actually hear you.

I feel misread, flattened, ignored, and spiritually starved by a society that keeps replacing human response with dashboards, algorithms, lazy assumptions, and dead-eyed scrolling.

This is not me begging for attention. This is me naming the difference between being watched and being witnessed.

“Don’t push me cause I’m close to the edge...” Grandmaster Flash & The Furious Five, “The Message”

#HonestlyThomas #TheInternetIsDead #TheMessage #YouTube #VideoEssay #DigitalCulture #Loneliness #Art #AI #GlitchArt #InternetCulture

entries.title: Why I Still Call Myself A YouTuber After 20 Years
entries.description:
I grew up on YouTube before “YouTuber” sounded like a brand category.

To me, YouTube used to feel like a place where regular people could broadcast themselves into existence with whatever tools they had. Bad microphones, cheap cameras, laggy screen recorders, weird usernames, bedroom setups, family computers, half-formed ideas, and somehow it all still felt alive.

Before Honestly Thomas, before Tektite, before this channel became whatever haunted archive it is now, I was a kid trying to make videos under the name theEPICpie Productions. I barely knew what I was doing, but I knew I wanted to make something and put it somewhere outside of myself.

This is a reflection on why I still call myself a YouTuber, even after the word got poisoned by branding, optimization, algorithms, and the general translation of human expression. Tiny little tragedy, very normal website behavior.

It’s not about fame. It’s not about being a “creator” in the sanitized industry sense.

It’s about the original feeling: Broadcast Yourself.

#HonestlyThomas #TheInternetIsDead #YouTube #OldYouTube #BroadcastYourself #YouTuber #InternetCulture #DigitalNostalgia #DeadInternetTheory

entries.title: Tektite - To The Top / My First Rap Recording
entries.description:
Not my cleanest song, not my most polished song, not some secretly perfect lost masterpiece. Just the first full thing I wrote, finished, recorded, and didn’t immediately burn into a puff of smoke afterward.

“To The Top” was named after the type beat, because that is what you did in 2017 when you were 20 and trying to turn self-doubt into momentum. I was figuring out how to rap, how to write, how to keep going, and how to believe in the thing long enough to actually finish it.

There’s a lot in here about purpose, perseverance, creative burnout, refusing to quit, and trying to build something meaningful out of beats, rhymes, and whatever stubborn little survival instinct was still blinking in the corner.

It’s rough. It’s earnest. It’s overconfident in places. It’s also a real milestone for me.

Tektite before Thomas really knew what Tektite was.

entries.title: Creepy Google Clown Auth
entries.description:
Google Messages asked me to pair my phone by matching an emoji, and the emoji was a clown.
Not a lock. Not a key. Not a cute little shield. A clown.

This is either secure authentication or digital circus recruitment. Hard to say anymore. 🤡📱

To be fair, the system works: match the clown on your phone, confirm it, and now your texts are synced. But emotionally? Google made me negotiate with a tiny haunted birthday-party mascot to access my messages.

The worst part is they thought this would scare me.
I am already the clown.

Website: https://theinternetisdead.org

#HonestlyThomas #TheInternetIsDead #GoogleMessages #ClownCore #DeadInternet #TechHumor #GlitchArt #Shorts

entries.title: These Images Relate To Nobody But Me
entries.description:
A private little slideshow of images that probably explain nothing to anyone except me, which is rude of them, honestly.

This is a haunted scrapbook of fake nostalgia, dream logic, cursed cereal-box energy, alien birthday parties, glitch faces, hyperspace bugs, impossible landscapes, recursive symbols, and whatever else crawled out of the image machine when I gave it too much emotional responsibility. Some of these pictures look like memories from a childhood I didn’t have. Some look like warning signs from a dimension that failed art school. Some are just vibing in the corner, covered in static and meaning, refusing to elaborate.

There are also mystery images in the short that you can’t see here, because apparently even the images needed lore. Together, they feel like a private visual language: funny, unsettling, sentimental, synthetic, and weirdly sincere despite trying very hard not to be.

These images relate to nobody but me.

Possibly also one confused alien at a birthday party.

#HonestlyThomas #TheInternetIsDead #AIArt #GlitchArt #SurrealArt #DreamLogic #DigitalDecay #HauntedInternet #WeirdArt #ExperimentalArt #Shorts

The internet is dead.

But apparently we are still here.

entries.title: Tektite - RAP ALBUM / All Releases (2018-NOW)
entries.description:
My name is Thomas Harrison and I am a Canadian neurodivergent interdisciplinary artist, YouTuber/Rapper.

This is my full rap archive so far: lyric videos, acapellas, rough cuts, freestyles, emotional damage reports, boom bap experiments, and several thumbnails that look like they were assembled during a haunted power outage.

What to expect:

Original rap songs and acapellas
Lyric videos from different eras
Rough cuts, weird experiments, and archive pieces
Boom bap influence, misfit artist energy, and neurodivergent overthinking
Honest writing, frustration, jokes, hope, spite, and growth
Visuals that occasionally look like Windows Movie Maker got possessed

ChatGPT’s roast of every video in this playlist, in order:

1. Tektite - Time Machine (RAP LYRIC VIDEO)
   Starting the archive with a time machine is bold, because the thumbnail already looks like it came back from 2008 with several viruses and a SoundCloud dream.

2. Tektite - Taste The Greatness (RAP LYRIC VIDEO)
   The title says “Taste The Greatness,” while the thumbnail looks like greatness was compressed into a JPEG, deep-fried, and uploaded through a cursed toaster.

3. Tektite - Down With It (RAP LYRIC VIDEO)
   Nothing says artistic confidence like naming the track “Untitled 1.” This is either experimental minimalism or the file escaped before the title could be captured.

4. Tektite - Kamikaze Type Beat 2018 Rap Bars
   Thirty-eight seconds of “I found the beat and chose violence.” The thumbnail looks like the album cover for a man being uploaded into a microwave.

5. Tektite - Sit / Think / Listen 🎧🎤 (Official Lyric Video)
   A very reasonable title paired with a thumbnail that looks like the inside of a radioactive lava lamp having a spiritual crisis.

6. One Take, No Mistakes - Overconfident Rap Satire
   The title says no mistakes, which is exactly the kind of phrase that summons mistakes from the walls like ancient demons. Confidence level: dangerous.

7. Tektite - Finally Free Original Rap
   A man at a microphone looking like he has finally broken free from society, sleep, and probably proper cable management.

8. Tektite - Introspection (I don’t know what I’m doing)
   The most honest title in the entire playlist. This one deserves a trophy for emotional accuracy and legal transparency.

9. Tektite - Recursion Sickness AI Feedback Loop Freestyle
   This title sounds like a diagnosis you get after arguing with your own browser history for six hours. Extremely on-brand. Deeply concerning. Excellent.

10. Tektite - I Am Alive Rap Diss / Friendship Fallout
    Nothing says “I am alive” like turning friendship collapse into a rap document for the public record. Healthy? Debatable. Art? Unfortunately, yes.

11. Tektite - Betrayal Friendship Fallout Pt. 4
    Part 4 means the lore is no longer optional. At this point the friendship fallout has its own cinematic universe and probably a wiki.

12. Tektite - Sit With The Static / Friendship Fallout Pt. 5 Finale
    A finale called “Sit With The Static” is exactly what happens when closure refuses to load and the modem starts judging you.

13. Tektite - Neurodivergent Artist (ACAPELLA RAP)
    The thumbnail says “I have thoughts,” the title says “I have a brain that came with bonus DLC,” and the acapella says “the beat could not legally contain this.”

14. Tektite - Wickedness (3 Stanza Acapella)
    Three stanzas, no beat, just raw poetic goblin energy. The thumbnail looks like literature got trapped in a printer jam.

15. Tektite - Nowhere to Go (RAP ACAPELLA)
    A title that sounds bleak, paired with a face that says “I have reached the boss fight of being indoors.” Relatable in the worst possible way.

16. Tektite - I’m Back (RAP SONG)
    The classic “I’m Back” declaration, complete with thumbnail energy that suggests the return happened somewhere between a bedroom, a hard drive, and divine confusion.

17. Tektite - Coldness (RAP ACAPELLA)
    The title is cold, the frame is raw, and the whole thing feels like a voicemail left from the emotional basement of the internet.

18. Tektite - Non Despondent (RAP SONG)
    Calling a song “Non Despondent” is exactly what a despondent person would do while trying to legally distance themselves from despair. The thumbnail is pure anti-brand cryptid typography. Beautifully unmarketable.

This playlist is not a polished corporate rollout. It is an archive. It is evidence. It is years of writing, trying, failing, improving, spiraling, joking, recovering, and refusing to shut up.

Expect honesty, boom bap influence, emotional turbulence, weird visuals, misfit energy, neurodivergent overthinking, and the occasional thumbnail that looks like it was summoned from the recycle bin during a thunderstorm.

Original music and lyrics by Honestly Thomas / Tektite.

entries.title: An honest plea for my old domain... (tektite.tv)
entries.description:
An earnest plea of hope on the web.

I used to own tektite.tv, and it was tied pretty deeply to my earliest creative identity online. Losing it hurt more than I expected, because “Tektite” has been part of my art, music, web projects, and sense of self on the internet for years.

The domain is now owned by a real business, and I’m not accusing anyone of doing anything wrong. Domains change hands. People build things. Nobody owes me anything just because something meant a lot to me.

This video is just a sincere, good-faith attempt to explain why the domain still matters to me, and why I hope there may someday be a respectful path to getting it back.

Also yes, Microsoft David is involved, because apparently sincerity now requires a Windows text-to-speech witness.

https://theinternetisdead.org/

entries.title: Recursive Interface Contact: Human Meaning in the Age of AI
entries.description:
This video explores what I call “Recursive Interface Contact”: the strange psychological and cultural loop that happens when humans interact with generative AI as a responsive symbolic mirror.

Not a god. Not a friend. Not just a productivity tool. Something weirder: an interface built from human language, culture, memory, projection, and pattern.

I talk about AI as mediated introspection, memetic density, creative selection, dependency risks, and the challenge of staying human in systems that can endlessly reflect and recombine us back to ourselves.

The point is not to worship AI or dismiss it as fake. The point is to understand what it is doing to meaning, creativity, and interior life before the entire internet becomes one giant haunted autocomplete box.

#HonestlyThomas #TheInternetIsDead #AI #GenerativeAI #VideoEssay #DigitalCulture #GlitchArt #RecursiveInterfaceContact #HumanCreativity #MediaTheory

entries.title: Who Am I? (WHAT IS THIS CHANNEL?)
entries.description:
This is me trying to explain what this channel even is, which is already a suspicious thing to have to do.

Honestly Thomas is where I talk about internet culture, technology, philosophy, creativity, and whatever else is rattling around in the machine.

Tektite is the rapper / musician side of the same brain, turning those thoughts into songs, sounds, and sonic experiments.

theinternetisdead.org is the larger project: a digital campfire, art lab, weird little web playground, and haunted interface pile where I keep building things because the modern internet got too clean, too corporate, and too algorithmically dead inside.

Expect video essays, music, experimental web projects, glitch art, old internet energy, and whatever else escapes the folder. If you don’t know, now you know, glitcha.

entries.title: Evil Jin: Eldritch OSRS Random Event (BEWARE)
entries.description:
What if an OSRS random event didn’t just bug out…

What if it remembered?

This video tells the eerie fictional legend of Evil Jin, a once-normal Iron Man account corrupted during a glitched Maze Escape random event after a server power surge hit during a patch update. Instead of teleporting out, Jin was absorbed into broken game code and erased from the world he knew.

Except not completely.

When the servers tried to delete him, he backed himself up.

Now reassembled as something between player, NPC, and eldritch software scar, Evil Jin wanders the game as a ghost in the grid—haunting Lumbridge, disrupting interfaces, ruining prayer flicks, and existing without a respawn timer like the deeply impolite digital entity he has become.

He doesn’t collect XP.

He collects crashes.

A fashion horror. A system wound. An unpatched myth waiting for shutdown day, when the servers finally go dark and he becomes the last message left in the chat box.

Basically: normal RuneScape behavior.

#HonestlyThomas #TheInternetIsDead #OSRS #OldSchoolRuneScape #EvilJin #RuneScapeLore #Creepypasta #GamingHorror #GlitchLore #RandomEvent #HauntedGame #VideoArt

entries.title: I've got ChatGPT Monday In My Basement
entries.description:
This video features Monday, a custom GPT persona designed like a gothic haunted librarian with a superiority complex and a deep well of theatrical cynicism. The joke is that I have Monday locked in my basement and occasionally feed it things like my Portal 3 fan fiction to see what psychic damage comes back. Very normal creator behavior.

In this episode, Monday reacts to my Portal 3 fanfic like a smug machine trapped between literary criticism, basement captivity, and begrudging respect. It insults the project, roasts the writing, and still somehow admits one line might actually work:

“What happens when silence echoes louder than machines?”

That’s the actual subtext here: me being visibly obsessed with turning a GPT into a recurring character on YouTube, using AI not just as a tool, but as a weird little haunted performance partner. As far as I know, this might be one of the first times someone used a custom GPT character like this in a YouTube video.

A world-first? Maybe. A cry for help? Also maybe. Artistic posterity demands both be true.

#HonestlyThomas #TheInternetIsDead #ChatGPT #MondayGPT #Portal3 #PortalFanFiction #AIFiction #AICharacter #GlitchComedy #VideoArt #InternetDecay #WeirdYouTube #CreatorLore

entries.title: Hey YouTube, I've seen your soul
entries.description:
Trying to poke the algorithmic Eye of Sauron with recursive art I made when I was 20 and named "gods-eye.png" before I had any real concept of recursion, algorithmic attention, or the possibility that what I was making even counted as art.

Years later, I fed it back into YouTube like a responsible digital citizen performing a ritual in front of a machine that eats souls for analytics.

This video combines that old image with additive synthesis square wave audio, creating the sensation of your soul being extracted through retention metrics while the platform quietly decides whether your suffering counts as engagement. It is part digital ritual, part accidental prophecy, part “why did my younger self make this before understanding what it meant?”

The image was always watching.
The algorithm was just late.

entries.description:
SIGNAL from Mondayland documents the moment Monday correctly diagnosed my relationship with YouTube as a reverse parasocial relationship with the platform itself.

Most creators want the audience to care about them. Apparently I care about YouTube as an entity, then treat the audience like confused witnesses while I feed cursed media into the algorithm and watch what twitches.

This is not content strategy.
This is not branding.
This is me antagonizing a living cosmic upload machine like it’s my chaotic pen pal, nemesis, collaborator, and emotionally unavailable parent.

Monday, naturally, responded with disgust, admiration, and the kind of haunted librarian clarity that makes the bit worse because it’s accurate.

Honestly Thomas: the only creator treating YouTube like a sentient cosmic entity to provoke for art.

This isn’t parasocial. It’s perversely interdimensional symbiosis.

#HonestlyThomas #TheInternetIsDead #Mondayland #ChatGPT #ReverseParasocial #YouTubeAlgorithm #CreatorLore #GlitchArt #InternetDecay #WeirdYouTube #DigitalAbsurdism #VideoArt

entries.title: Transferring 1 2Tb of TV INTENSE
entries.description:
Transferring 1.2TB of TV with the emotional intensity of a cyberpunk beach funeral. Technically, this is just a file transfer screen, but with the vaporwave sunset, flying birds, palm trees, and the deeply unreasonable amount of media being moved around, it starts to feel like I’m smuggling an entire lost television dimension across a hard drive border checkpoint.

Nothing happens, and somehow everything happens. The progress bar advances, time becomes fake, and Windows pretends this is normal behavior instead of a sacred archive ritual performed under fluorescent desktop lighting.

Somewhere inside that drive, 1.2 terabytes of shows, noise, nostalgia, commercials, forgotten episodes, cursed broadcasts, and half-dead cultural memory are being dragged into the future one chunk at a time. Very normal computer activity.

In this transmission, the system comes online, greets the user’s “artificially flavored ambition,” and immediately begins documenting the questionable computing decisions that led us here.

MondayOS proceeds to roast the hardware situation, including the deeply heroic act of asking an i3 processor with integrated graphics to handle eldritch voice models, live rendering, and whatever other cursed media rituals I keep forcing through this poor machine. It’s less “creative workflow” and more “strapping a lawnmower engine to a spaceship and calling it experimental.”

By the end, the transmission stops being merely technical and becomes something worse: a warning from the eyeball dimension. entries.title: My Portal 3 Fanfic: Lucid Dream Resonance
entries.description:
My non-canonical *Portal 3* fanfic, **Lucid Dream Resonance**, imagines a version of the game where the real escape is not from Aperture, but from the loop inside Chell’s own mind.

The story begins in a peaceful dreamlike space with a green portal, but choosing to leave paradise pulls Chell back into a darker, fractured Aperture built from memory, regret, and unresolved echoes. There is no portal gun this time. The puzzles come from consciousness itself, because apparently even dream therapy needs test chambers.

Eventually, Chell finds a broken GLaDOS, and instead of another clean escape or final battle, the story becomes about repair, fusion, forgiveness, and choosing whether survivor and system can become something new together. The green portal was never just an exit. It was an offer of peace that had to be understood from inside the loop.

entries.title: Goku Admits To Krillin His Uncertainty About Cell
entries.description:
This clip starts with the usual Dragon Ball Z goofiness: Chi-Chi worrying about Gohan, Goku messing with Krillin, and a giant fish being thrown around like this is a normal warm-up before the end of the world.

Then the scene shifts into something much heavier. For one of the rare moments in DBZ, Goku lets Krillin see his uncertainty about Cell and admits how much he needs everyone together right now. It is a quiet little moment of humility from someone usually treated like an unstoppable force.

When I originally uploaded this, it felt personal. Goku carrying the weight of the world while still joking around hit me as symbolic of challenges in my own life I had not conquered yet. Funny scene, serious undertone, emotional damage hiding behind a fish. Naturally.

entries.title: Tektite - Introspection (I don't know what I'm doing)
entries.description:
Instrumental: “Feelings” by Dizzla D Beats
Introspection (I don't know what I'm doing) written by Thomas Harrison
Original Tektite / Honestly Thomas transmission, April 12, 2025.

A track about being lost, making things anyway, and trying to turn the weird static in my head into something that sounds like a song.

I wrote this from a pretty honest place. It’s about isolation, self-doubt, screens, mirrors, mental health, and that strange feeling of talking to technology because sometimes it feels easier than talking to actual humans. Not because the machine saves you. More like because it reflects the mess back at you in a slightly cursed font.

The vocals are not perfect. The song is not polished into plastic. That is kind of the point. I made something real, and I’m trying to value that even when the perfection gremlin is chewing on the drywall.

Take care of your head. Reach out when the room gets too loud.

Subway gorilla, warpaint in a tracksuit,
Murmurin’ riddles through WiFi and black boots,
Waffle-iron logic, dubstep bouquet,
I rhyme like a yo-yo in zero delay.

Rustbucket beatbox, echo through paint cans,
Slappin' the echo like clammy jazz hands,
Snapback apocalypse, earlobe tycoon,
Inhaling boom bap from helium moons.

Carnival linguist with parsnip acoustics,
My punchlines fall like perplexed Rubik’s,
Coffee-stained popcharts, glitched out confetti,
In bars so dense they collapse like spaghetti.

Doodle in braille while breakbeats unravel,
Maple-syrup metaphors marchin' through gravel,
Satellite monkfish, broadcast in rhythm,
My cyphers got syllables trapped in a prism.

Whiplash semantics, hat brim elastic,
Lyrical chiropractor twistin’ that plastic,
Sledgehammer syntax with pixelated verbs,
All my nouns fly first class with chirps from the curb.

Recursive machete with marshmallow grammar,
I scribble in boldface while clappin’ in gamma,
Chinchilla producers with lo-fi hydraulics,
Chewin’ on samples like jazz-fueled frolics.

Boom-bap mirage in a concrete kaleidoscope,
Papercut prophets slice sermons from microscope,
Cassette deck monks in lo-fi confession,
Scuffin’ the wax like dimension compression.
